summary: Tested StakeChess critical bugs: 1) Piece movement via API works correctly, 2) Timer logic is correctly implemented (black timer only starts after white's first move). However, FRONTEND DRAG-AND-DROP IS NOT WORKING - pieces cannot be moved via UI drag operations despite backend API working correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Drag-and-drop piece movement does not trigger onPieceDrop callback. Pieces cannot be moved via UI despite arePiecesDraggable=true and backend API working correctly.
ui_bugs.details: Tested multiple drag approaches (mouse events, pointer events, locator.drag_to) - none trigger the onPieceDrop callback. The react-chessboard component (v5.10.0) renders pieces with cursor:grab but drag operations don't work. This is the main bug preventing gameplay.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend drag-and-drop does not communicate with backend move API

passed_tests: Backend API /api/games/{game_id}/move works correctly (verified via curl)
Game join flow works (status changes to active)
Move validation works (cannot move out of turn)
Timer logic is correct - white timer runs on white's turn, black timer only runs after white's first move
All 15 backend API endpoints working (100% success rate)
All 11 pytest game move tests passed
Game state persists correctly in database
Frontend loads correctly with chessboard displayed
Move history displays correctly in UI
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix react-chessboard drag-and-drop - onPieceDrop callback is not being triggered when pieces are dragged
Investigate react-chessboard v5.10.0 compatibility with React 19
Consider adding click-to-move as fallback (click source square, then click target square)
Check if there's a CSS/z-index issue preventing drag events from reaching the chessboard component
critical_code_review_comments: The onPieceDrop function in Game.jsx (lines 259-360) is correctly implemented but never gets called during drag operations
arePiecesDraggable prop evaluates to true for active games but drag events don't trigger the callback
Timer logic (lines 215-251) is correctly implemented - black timer only decrements when totalMoves >= 1
WebSocket connection fails due to proxy issues but HTTP move endpoint works correctly
seed_data_creation: Used existing test game game_74a9f7253ce8 with admin vs testplayer
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend move API works perfectly via curl/HTTP requests. The issue is specifically with the react-chessboard component's drag-and-drop not triggering the onPieceDrop callback. Timer bug is already fixed in the code. Focus testing on the Chessboard component integration.

bug_1_piece_movement.symptom: Pieces cannot be moved during a game via UI drag-and-drop
bug_1_piece_movement.root_cause: The react-chessboard component's onPieceDrop callback is not being triggered when pieces are dragged. The component renders with cursor:grab and arePiecesDraggable=true, but drag events don't reach the callback.
reproduction_steps: 3. Try to drag any white piece (e.g., knight from g1 to f3)
4. Observe that the piece doesn't move and no API call is made
potential_fixes: Check react-chessboard v5.10.0 compatibility with React 19
Add click-to-move as alternative input method
Check for CSS pointer-events issues on parent containers

bug_2_timer.symptom: Timer bug where black's timer starts before white's first move
bug_2_timer.root_cause: ALREADY FIXED - Timer logic at lines 215-251 correctly checks totalMoves >= 1 before decrementing black's timer
